residual_risks: Milestone-7 remains intentionally open and row 7 stays NO in the mechanism table.

The accepted result freezes only the current specialization-based row-6 lowerability boundary; any broader runtime-polymorphism support, public lowering surface, fallback lowering lane, or second executable IR will require new evidence and a later accepted round or roadmap revision.

focused_row_6_guard.command: cabal test mlf2-test --test-show-details=direct --test-options='--match "/Repository guardrails/polymorphism-erasure and lowerability contract stays explicit without widening the backend boundary/"'

evidence_summary: Reviewed the canonical round-227 worktree against master commit b4e239c5d6fafd55ed56ed81154ec4e3faf79ac8, where HEAD, master, and merge-base all match and the live round exists as an uncommitted worktree diff. The implementation-owned merge payload stays limited to docs/architecture.md, docs/backend-native-pipeline.md, src/MLF/Backend/IR.hs, src/MLF/Backend/Convert.hs, src/MLF/Backend/LLVM/Lower.hs, docs/plans/2026-05-02-backend-ir-executable-boundary-mechanism-table.md, test/BackendLLVMSpec.hs, and test/RepoGuardSpec.hs, while orchestrator/state.json, the pointer stubs, and the active roadmap/round lineage artifacts are controller-owned activation and review bookkeeping. The round makes the row-6 polymorphism lowerability boundary explicit across the synchronized contract surfaces, keeps checked Backend.IR permissive for BackendTyAbs and BackendTyApp while restricting LLVM/native emission to the specialization-based lowerable subset, adds focused supported and rejection coverage in BackendLLVMSpec plus the exact repository guard, flips only mechanism-table row 6 to YES while preserving rows 1-5 at YES and row 7 at NO, passes the focused row-6 slices, and passes cabal build all && cabal test with 2352 examples and 0 failures. The inherited one-backend-IR, eager-runtime, no-lazy-STG, no-public-lower-IR, and no-fallback boundaries remain intact, and the round does not widen into row-7 closeout work, runtime polymorphism, or a broader public lowering surface.
